steps.title: Spot the replicated pattern
steps.content: The passage establishes a language-region correlation in China (Mandarin in the north, Cantonese and Fuzhounese in the south) and then shows the same correlation emerging in NYC (Mandarin speakers from northern China settling in one area, southern language speakers in another). This replication of the original-country pattern is the signal.

steps.title: Connect to the answer
steps.content: Choice D states that regional language correlations can replicate in a new country when people emigrate—precisely what the passage shows. The other choices make unrelated claims about how rapidly languages change, how they differ across locations, and whether country size correlates with language diversity. That matches choice D — D is correct.
